content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660              322-5901           322-6441              322-6444

April 6, 1981


Honorable Dawn B. Girard
Municipal Court
Berkeley-Albany Judicial District
2120 Grove Street
Berkeley, CA 94704

Dear Judge Girard:

Ms. Hughes has requested that I respond to your letter
of March 19, 1981. The Political Reform Act requires that
all judges of courts of record file Statements of Economic
Interests. Government Code Sections 87200-87210.1/ The
Statement must disclose the precise location of all interests
in real property which exceed $1,000, other than the filer's
principal residence. Section 87206(c), (f). There is no
provision under which the Fair Political Practices Commission
can grant an exemption to this requirement.

I would like to point out that in disclosing an interest
in real property, it is not necessary to disclose the street
address if the assessor's parcel number is given. Furthermore,
it is not necessary to indicate the use to which your real
property is put. Without such an explanation, there is, of
course, nothing on the Statement which indicates that you
hold one of your properties as a vacation residence rather
than as an investment.

The objections you raise to disclosure have been considered
and rejected in connection with disclosure provisions for
federal judges. E.g. Duplantier v. United States (5th Cir.,
1979) 606 F.2d 654, 669-671, cert. den. 49 U.S.L.W. 3493
(1/12/81). You might also want to look at the California
cases on this point. Hayes v. Wood (1979) 25 Cal. 3d 772;
County of Nevada v. McMillen (1974) 11 Cal. 3d 662,; City of
Carmel-by-the-Sea v. Young (1970) 2 Cal. 3d 259.


1/ All statutory references are to the Government
Code.

Honorable Dawn B. Girard
April 6, 1981
Page Two


I hope this answers your questions as to your filing
obligations. If you have further questions, please feel
free to call me at (916) 322-5901.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh

MUNICIPAL COURT
BERKELEY-ALBANY JUDICIAL DISTRICT
BERKELEY, CALIFORNIA 94704

CHAMBERS OF
JUDGE DAWN B. GIRARD
March 19, 1981


Alice Hughes
Documents Examiner
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Re: State of Economic Interests

Dear Ms. Hughes:

     I did not indicate the address of our vacation cabin
inasmuch as my family and I spend weekends there. It is a
second home. As a judge of a trial court, I believe it is not
necessary to indicate home addresses, for very good reasons.
Many of the defendants and litigants who appear before us
cannot be depended upon to limit their contacts with judges to
the Courthouse. In fact, many pose a serious threat to the
judges. Therefore, it is imperative that home addresses not be
divulged and made public information.

     If the information requested were kept in a confidential file, not subject to public inspection, I would happily
provide the address. Since it is open to public inspection
upon request, I decline, for the sake of the safety of my family
and myself, to provide the address.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        Dawn B. Girard

DBG:mc

cc: Clerk of the Court
